programmes.show_description: Never Say Alan Again: Comedy classic. Alan plans to watch all the Bond movies back to back and becomes jealous when Michael gets a new friend.
I Know What Alan Did Last Summer: Steve Coogan's comedy classic. Alan is anxious about a visit from the Inland Revenue and lies to his girlfriend about Bono.
Alan Wide Shut: Classic comedy with Steve Coogan. Alan's book is due to be pulped and he's clearly feeling the strain at Lynn's baptism.
The guests sorting the fact from fiction with Rob Brydon, Lee Mack and David Mitchell this time are Sophie Ellis-Bextor, Loyiso Gola, Jason Manford and Briony May Williams.
Rob Brydon is back in the host's chair as Lee Mack and David Mitchell are joined by Pam Ayres, Sarah Kendall, Richard Osman and Kiell Smith-Bynoe this time.
Coral Triangle: Simon Reeve voyages across the Coral Triangle in the south-western Pacific, surrounded by the Philippines, Malaysia and Indonesia.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Patagonia: Simon Reeve treks across the high mountains and treacherous ice fields of Patagonia, getting close to pumas and riding with gauchos, the cowboys of South America.
Yeovil: The volatile reality series following rival auction experts who try to snare big prizes for low prices. A slice of Vegas is among the discoveries. Dan pushes Nat too far.
Gwent: The hugely popular reality series comes to the UK. Heavy D confuses the other bidders, Sean and indeed himself! A 'classic car' could well turn out to be an old banger.
Officers in Brownsville process an American citizen who crossed over the Rio Grande 20 years ago - and discover the reason he didn't want to come back.
A Cuban woman crosses the bridge in the hope of being granted political asylum in America, while officers in Laredo stop three students carrying something unusual in their car.
Chris, Freddie and Paddy buy delivery vans on a budget and deploy science to find the best family car - later, Paddy heads to Italy to test out Ferrari's beautiful Daytona SP3.
Harry Enfield joins Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond, and Jason Dawe for a chat as they review the Citroen Berlingo, the Ford GT40, and the Mazda 6.
Paul and Bob head to Norfolk and attempt to ensnare a big pike - something that has eluded them so far on their fishing trips - before Bob reveals a hidden trick to Paul.
Paul and Bob return to the river where they first went fishing together after Bob's heart surgery - the Test in Hampshire - which is renowned as the home for fly-fishing.
Pub Grub: The bikers head to the pub to explore the story behind the boom in pub food and snacks and recreate mouthwatering dishes that would make any publican proud.
Preserving: The bikers journey through time, celebrating British food. Here they explore how some of our tastiest treats have evolved from a need to preserve.
Footballer Jill Scott joins Robson on a great outdoor adventure in North Yorkshire where they attempt paddleboard yoga and visit a walled garden.
Patagonia: Simon Reeve treks across the high mountains and treacherous ice fields of Patagonia, getting close to pumas and riding with gauchos, the cowboys of South America.
Coral Triangle: Simon Reeve voyages across the Coral Triangle in the south-western Pacific, surrounded by the Philippines, Malaysia and Indonesia.

programmes.show_name: Special Ops: To Catch a Criminal
programmes.show_description: Operation Earth: An RSPCA Special Ops team investigate an international criminal puppy farming operation, and Lancashire officers prosecute an owner for animal cruelty.

programmes.show_name: Special Ops: To Catch a Criminal
programmes.show_description: Operation Harlequin: The Serious Fraud Office go undercover to investigate a suspect holiday home company offering exclusive residences in the sun-drenched Caribbean.

programmes.show_name: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programmes.show_description: Martin Clunes is host as journalist Lyse Doucet and comedian Chloe Petts join team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop to delve into the news.
Rubbish: Sandi Toksvig talks rubbish with Alan Davies, Bridget Christie, Johnny Vegas and Holly Walsh.
Topical teasing as Hugh Dennis, Andy Parsons, Rob Beckett, Gary Delaney, Sara Pascoe and Josh Widdicombe dredge up the week's political pratfalls. Dara O Briain presents.
Angela Barnes, Miles Jupp, Romesh Ranganathan and Josh Widdicombe join Dara and co to pitch their 'Commercials That Never Made It to Air'! Plus, all the usual topical mockery.
More cutting-edge stand-up from the iconic theatre in London. Angela Barnes hosts this edition of the show and welcomes comedians Sarah Keyworth and Tim Renkow to the stage.
All I Want for Christmas Is U: Sandi Toksvig is joined by Eshaan Akbar, Jo Brand, Jimmy Carr and Alan Davies for a festive special.
General Store: More comedic capers from the backwater town with a novel name. David comes up with a new business venture, and Alexis adjusts to her first day of school.
Motel Review: Charmingly quirky comedy. David meets a new friend while setting up his business, Johnny defends Alexis's schoolwork, and Moira deals with a difficult guest at the motel.
The Affair: Liaise once again with the multi-award-winning sitcom. Moira and Roland find themselves in a compromising situation, and Alexis drives a wedge between David and Patrick.
Sebastien Raine: Another edition of the all-embracing smash-hit comedy. David's ex comes to town to do a photoshoot for Moira, and Johnny and Roland think that Bob is cheating at poker.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.

programmes.show_name: Dave Gorman: Modern Life Is Goodish
programmes.show_description: I Like Hot Bananas: One very funny man and his laptop. Dave puts 'Greatest Hits' albums and superlative abuse under the spotlight. Warning: contains nude yo-yoing.
You Don't Get That In The Met: The drug squad arrest three men for dealing coke - one runs and is tackled. The squad also find hash in a bush at a beauty spot, and a man runs from a road check.
Welcome to Paradise: Customs officers rescue a man from the sea at night after he falls from a smuggler's boat, and the Drug Squad raid a suspected dealer's house.
Documentary series. A retooled grain train springs some leaks, Bernie's Streamliner festival faces disaster, and the Pichi Richi Explorer takes part in a crazy race.
Sardinia and Sicily: Rick Stein explores the food of the Mediterranean. In Sardinia, Rick watches the local fishermen catch mullet using age-old methods, then takes a ferry to Sicily.
Money and guns belonging to a cartel are found in a car, an illegal shipment of exotic reptiles is discovered in a van, and a man is caught smuggling medication.
An armed robber on the run is captured at the border, a drunk driver causes a three-car crash on a bridge, and cocaine is found hidden inside office furniture.
Miner: Guy goes to work one mile below the North Sea in the UK's last deep mine - it's punishing work in blistering heat, and very real dangers lurk around every corner.
Clarkson and chums hope to end the chaos that snow can bring to Britain, using a combine harvester, basic engineering skills and a quick trip to Norway. Amber Heard guests.
Jeremy races the new Jaguar XJ against something even bigger and more powerful - the rotation of the Earth! The boys egg on John Prescott to take to the track for a few laps.
Slow Food: The bikers celebrate British food. For something delicious, it's worth using the best ingredients and cooking them to perfection - however long it takes.
Sardinia and Sicily: Rick Stein explores the food of the Mediterranean. In Sardinia, Rick watches the local fishermen catch mullet using age-old methods, then takes a ferry to Sicily.

programmes.show_name: Would I Lie To You At Christmas
programmes.show_description: Sorting the fact from the fiction in this festive special are guests Jim Broadbent, Ardal O'Hanlon, Rose Matafeo and Angela Rippon.
TV presenters Alex Jones and Karim Zeroual, comedian Jessica Knappett, and Judge Rob Rinder think they've got what it takes to take home one of the Pointless star's prizes this time.
Retired athletes Steve Cram and Jeanette Kwakye hope that they won't be for the high jump when they join comedians Lloyd Griffith and Josie Long on the lovable quiz show.
All I Want for Christmas Is U: Sandi Toksvig is joined by Eshaan Akbar, Jo Brand, Jimmy Carr and Alan Davies for a festive special.

programmes.show_name: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programmes.show_description: Bill Bailey is host as comedian Daliso Chaponda and journalist Helen Lewis join team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop to delve into the news.
Shaqinahat: Greg Davies delivers angry appraisals from his comfy throne as Ed Gamble dons a baby's bonnet, Katy Wix makes Alex Horne wince, and Rose Matafeo does a spot of screaming.
